acceptance_targets: [chapter_02_prose_001] Pass if the revised material anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:159, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:293, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:417, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:443, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:483, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:517 uses the corrective 'Not ... / Because ... / This was not ...' structure in no more than one of those paragraphs and lets the others land through unqualified observation or action; fail if multiple revised paragraphs still rely on the same self-correcting template.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised material anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:159, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:293, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:417, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:443, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:483, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:517 uses the corrective 'Not ... / Because ... / This was not ...' structure in no more than one of those paragraphs and lets the others land through unqualified observation or action; fail if multiple revised paragraphs still rely on the same self-correcting template.
findings.problem: The chapter leans too often on a negation-then-correction sentence shape ('Not X. Not because Y. This was not that.') to interpret Lena's feeling. Used once or twice it is sharp; repeated across the chapter it becomes an audible prose tic and flattens the rhythm of discovery.
findings.rewrite_direction: Recast several of the corrective-negation clusters at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:159, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:293, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:361, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:417, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:443, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:483, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:517 into direct positive image, action, or bodily inference. Keep the construction only where it earns special force instead of making it the chapter's default interpretive rhythm.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Focus on prose and copyedit finish: diction precision, rhythm control, sentence-level clarity, grammar, line-level polish without flattening style, and explicit aesthetic-risk audit (sanitization is the primary risk — do not soften or euphemize; push toward creative risk and specificity).
revision_pass.label: prose/copyedit fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the embarrassing specifics: transfer amounts, body awkwardness, neighborhood petty detail, ugly motives, and the unstable overlap between care and control. When a choice arises between a safer generalized line and a sharper socially revealing one, choose the sharper line unless it breaks character truth.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene reaches sex, money shame, betrayal, or bodily disgust and the prose flinches into summary or tasteful fog, revise toward concrete action, sensory residue, and social consequence.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are normal in almost all spoken English here; full forms are reserved for emphasis, class performance, or moments of deliberate control.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate compressed one-scene chapters with socially dense multi-scene chapters; follow hot chapters with quiet domestic ones, let comic humiliation sit beside grief, and vary dialogue density so no two consecutive chapters share the same pulse.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary, unsanitized, class-attentive English with enough lyric lift to surprise but not enough polish to float above the bodies and bills in the room.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: tight third person centered on Lena, with no head-hopping; June and Eli are knowable only through what Lena hears, sees, infers, and later uncovers in artifacts.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Supple, pressure-sensitive sentences that can hold embarrassment and desire in the same line, then snap short at moments of exposure or financial pain.
